hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: Buyers are suddenly confident the shutdown ends by March 31, jumping this 10 points in 24 hours, probably pricing in either a last-minute GOP cave or Democratic concessions as the deadline approaches. At 45%, the market is underweighting the political incentive for both parties to avoid a government closure before an election year, making YES the obvious lean.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Odds Jump 10 Points. Market Pricing In Reality Check.
daily_take.deck: The DHS shutdown resolution market ripped 10 points in 24 hours, now sitting at 45% odds of ending after March 31. The move signals traders are finally pricing in Congress' actual dysfunction rather than assuming another last-minute deal. At 45%, you're getting fair value on "no deal by March 31," but the real edge is watching whether this momentum holds or corrects on any procedural noise from Capitol Hill.

sidebar.headline: CDU's 27-point crater suggests market overshot on Baden-Württemberg. Watch for mean reversion if polling steadies.
sidebar.label: CDU Seats Lead: 59.2%

sidebar.headline: Panama favored at 40% in Round 1, but down 22 points. Check if line moved on injury reports or weather, not fundamentals.
sidebar.label: Panama Win: 40.0%

sidebar.headline: Elon's OpenAI case holding at 41.5% despite recent legal filings. Flat action suggests market has priced in the litigation risk correctly, or it's asleep.
sidebar.label: Elon Wins: 41.5%
